commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0e8e89159022bfeb4b85016bb79a9eec31ef2284)
commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces the `programs.openclaw.documents` directory API with explicit `workspace.bootstrapFiles` and `workspace.files`, forces `skipBootstrap` for Nix-managed bootstrap files, and updates templates, docs, and module checks.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: PR head validates exact reserved names only, while the activation materializer removes and recopies every managed target. I did not run Nix checks, but the `workspace.files."memory/foo"` path follows directly from the inspected code.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A small validation fix is needed, but the remaining hard-break and `skipBootstrap` policy tradeoff requires maintainer judgment before merge.

target.title: Replace documents with workspace bootstrap files
target.body:
# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> If skipBootstrap = false, OpenClaw may create missing bootstrap files from bundled templates.  -> i think we should NEVER let openclaw do that because it fucks up the declarative/nix ownership for the future
>
> The tradeoff: it disables OpenClaw’s first-run BOOTSTRAP.md ritual for Nix-managed installs. I think that is correct for the declarative path. The template should create starter files in the repo instead. -> this is fine, the bootstrap ritual is known not working
>
> programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles = {
>   agents = ./workspace/AGENTS.md;
>   soul = ./workspace/SOUL.md;
>   tools = ./workspace/TOOLS.md;
>   identity = ./workspace/IDENTITY.md;
>   user = ./workspace/USER.md;
>
>   heartbeat = null;
> };
>
> programs.openclaw.workspace.files = {
>   "LORE.md" = ./workspace/LORE.md;
>   "PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md" = ./workspace/PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md;
> };
>
> -> explicit is better. zen of python. explicit over implicit.
>
> they have coding agents they can use them.

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex-unknown, running in the codex harness. Full environment + prompt history appear at the end._

# Changes
- Replaces `programs.openclaw.documents` with explicit `programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles` for `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and optional `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Adds `programs.openclaw.workspace.files` for extra Nix-managed workspace files that are not upstream bootstrap files.
- Forces `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true` when Nix manages bootstrap files, and fails eval if user config tries to set it false.
- Leaves `BOOTSTRAP.md` and `MEMORY.md` runtime-owned and blocks managing them through `workspace.files`.
- Strengthens the removed-`documents` migration failure: if the old directory is readable, the assertion names old non-bootstrap files such as `LORE.md` and `PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md` so users know to move them into `workspace.files`.
- Updates the agent-first template, README, golden-path docs, and focused module/activation checks.

# Tests
- PASS: `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- PASS: `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.workspace-materializer .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- PASS: `nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.hm-activation --accept-flake-config --no-link --dry-run`
- PASS: temp-copy `nixos-config` evals with `nix-openclaw` overridden to this branch and `ai-stack` overridden to a temp migrated copy:
  - `darwinConfigurations.aarch64-darwin` rendered `/Users/josh/.openclaw/workspace` with `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true`.
  - `darwinConfigurations.aarch64-darwin-openclaw-local` rendered `/Users/josh/.openclaw-test/workspace` with `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true`.
  - `darwinConfigurations.mac-mini` rendered `/Users/josh/.openclaw/workspace` with `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true`.
  - `nixosConfigurations.djtbot-1` rendered `/home/djtbot/.openclaw-prod/workspace` with `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true`.

# Risks
- Compatibility: this intentionally hard-breaks `programs.openclaw.documents`; existing users must migrate before evaluation succeeds.
- Optional old files are not preserved by magic. They stay Nix-managed only if users explicitly declare them under `workspace.files` or, for `HEARTBEAT.md`, `workspace.bootstrapFiles.heartbeat`.
- Companion config updates are needed in downstream configs that still set `documents`; temp-copy testing showed this affects both private host config and public `ai-stack` defaults.

# Follow-ups
- After this PR lands, migrate `ai-stack` to provide public bootstrap files without public `USER.md`.
- After `ai-stack` is migrated, migrate private `nixos-config` host modules to supply `USER.md` and host-specific extra workspace files.

# Prompt History

## Environment
Harness: codex
Model: unknown
Thinking level: unknown
Terminal: unknown
System: macOS 26.5

## Prompts
Prompt timestamps below use the PR-body update time because the Codex UI did not expose exact per-message timestamps in this thread. Prompts are preserved verbatim where they directly shaped this PR.

### 2026-06-05T20:57:11+02:00
```text
If skipBootstrap = false, OpenClaw may create missing bootstrap files from bundled templates.  -> i think we should NEVER let openclaw do that because it fucks up the declarative/nix ownership for the future

The tradeoff: it disables OpenClaw’s first-run BOOTSTRAP.md ritual for Nix-managed installs. I think that is correct for the declarative path. The template should create starter files in the repo instead. -> this is fine, the bootstrap ritual is known not working

programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles = {
  agents = ./workspace/AGENTS.md;
  soul = ./workspace/SOUL.md;
  tools = ./workspace/TOOLS.md;
  identity = ./workspace/IDENTITY.md;
  user = ./workspace/USER.md;

  heartbeat = null;
};

programs.openclaw.workspace.files = {
  "LORE.md" = ./workspace/LORE.md;
  "PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md" = ./workspace/PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md;
};

-> explicit is better. zen of python. explicit over implicit. 

they have coding agents they can use them. 

spin this up in a worktree and make a PR, use [$session-goal-writer](/nix/store/hcks99ivrjhg2pg09lc97cqhd2ixh1w1-source/skills/session-goal-writer/SKILL.md) , do multple passes of adversarial review
```

### 2026-06-05T20:57:11+02:00
```text
[P1] Previously Nix-managed optional files such as LORE.md or PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md can be removed from the managed workspace on upgrade unless users move them into workspace.files.

clawsweeper has a point here - what do you suggest?
```

### 2026-06-05T20:57:11+02:00
```text
also have we tested this against temp copy of my local flows? (don't mutate in place)
```

# AI Disclosure

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123
